title: CAAM test closure: truthful no-mock unit coverage and complete detailed-logging E2E coverage
description: Current truth audit says the broad claim is false. We do not currently have full no-mock unit coverage, and we do not currently have complete E2E integration coverage with verified detailed forensic logging across all required scenarios. This program closes the gap by: (1) stabilizing and truthifying the audit surfaces, (2) burning down broad mock/fake allowlist debt, (3) completing missing real-behavior unit/integration coverage, (4) finishing and hardening E2E scenario coverage, logging, and live validation, and (5) publishing fail-closed certification artifacts.

comments.author: AzureRidge
comments.text: Current evidence driving this program: (1) go test ./... and scripts/test_audit.sh are presently blocked by internal/e2e/workflows TestDaemonSignals cleanup failure; (2) critical package coverage is far from any truthful full-unit-coverage claim, including cmd/caam/cmd 42.8%% and internal/exec 0.0%%; (3) docs/testing/test_realism_allowlist.json currently broad-allowlists many core user-facing surfaces as boundary, so a repo-level claim of no-mock unit coverage would be false even before burn-down; (4) the repo has useful E2E harness logging, but complete scenario traceability and universal canonical-log validation are not closed yet; (5) live exhausted-account switching and live resume-correct-conversation proof are still separate bounded-live validation needs, not already-proven facts.

comments.author: GreenBrook
comments.text: 2026-03-10 truth refresh: the broad claim is still false. The repo still has no honest full no-mock unit-coverage certificate, the full suite still hits internal/e2e/workflows TestDaemonSignals, docs/testing/cli_scenario_traceability.md still reports 74 covered of 227 required scenarios, docs/testing/e2e_gap_closure_rollup.md still says not certifiable, and release attestation inputs are still mandatory. I added a deeper child lattice under bd-3fy.2.*, bd-3fy.3.*, and bd-3fy.4.* so work can proceed package-by-package, scenario-by-scenario, and artifact-by-artifact instead of hiding behind coarse top-level tasks.

title: Truth baseline and audit stabilization
description: Make the repo capable of saying what is true today even when parts of the suite fail. Current blockers include a failing internal/e2e workflow (TestDaemonSignals) and audit scripts that abort before emitting complete artifacts. This lane creates reliable baseline evidence for coverage, realism debt, E2E inventory, and logging completeness.

comments.author: AzureRidge
comments.text: This lane exists because current audit machinery does not reliably emit a full truth packet when part of the suite fails. The first failing condition today is TestDaemonSignals, which prevents test_audit from writing the artifacts that downstream gating depends on. Truth must be publishable even when truth is red.

title: Split audit generation from failing suite execution so artifacts still emit on partial failure
description: Refactor scripts/test_audit.sh and adjacent reporting so a single failing workflow test does not prevent coverage, realism, and E2E inventory artifacts from being written. Preserve explicit failure status while still publishing the first failing condition and the partial evidence pack.

title: Refresh realism truth: inventory broad allowlist exceptions and decide keep/narrow/remove
description: Capture the current effective no-mock truth, including the fact that docs/testing/test_realism_allowlist.json currently treats large core surfaces such as cmd/caam/cmd, internal/exec, internal/coordinator, authfile, authpool, identity, profile, and internal/e2e/workflows as boundary-allowed. Produce a decision ledger for each broad exception.

title: Refresh critical-package coverage ledger with current measured gaps
description: Record the current measured package coverage for the critical surfaces that invalidate any claim of full unit coverage today. Current evidence includes cmd/caam/cmd 42.8%, internal/exec 0.0%, internal/agent 70.9%, internal/authfile 75.6%, internal/identity 74.8%, internal/profile 78.3%, internal/refresh 78.7%, and internal/rotation 70.0%.

title: Refresh E2E inventory, traceability, and logging-completeness baseline
description: Capture which E2E workflows exist, which are passing, which scenarios are still open, and where logging is present but not formally validated. Include the currently failing TestDaemonSignals workflow and the existing gap-rollup / traceability baselines in the refreshed truth artifact set.

title: No-mock unit and integration closure
description: Close the gap between current broad boundary allowlist policy and the desired claim of full no-mock unit coverage. The goal is to replace package-wide exceptions with real-behavior fixtures and only narrowly justified, expiring exceptions where unavoidable.

comments.author: AzureRidge
comments.text: This lane exists because the current no-mock story is mostly policy fiction: package-wide allowlist entries mark cmd/caam/cmd, internal/exec, internal/coordinator, authfile, authpool, identity, profile, and internal/e2e/workflows as boundary-allowed. Until those broad exemptions are burned down and strict lint goes green without them, the repo cannot honestly claim full no-mock unit coverage.

comments.author: GreenBrook
comments.text: Decomposition note: this epic now has explicit child leaves for internal/exec fixture realism, cmd/caam/cmd temp-home/temp-vault command realism, and core auth/profile/rotation/ratelimit realism. The intent is to turn the broad no-mock claim into a package-by-package closure program with auditable removal of blanket waivers.

title: Replace internal/exec mock-heavy tests with real-behavior fixture coverage and raise coverage from 0%
description: internal/exec is a critical runtime path and currently measures 0.0% package coverage under go test -cover. Add real subprocess/PTY/profile fixtures and integration-style unit tests so the runtime handoff, resume, notifier, retry, and session-state behaviors are exercised without broad provider doubles where not strictly necessary.

title: Lay down reusable real subprocess, PTY, and profile fixtures for internal/exec
description: Create a shared fixture kit for internal/exec that uses real child processes, real PTY controllers where feasible, temp profile stores, temp vaults, and deterministic transcript capture. The goal is to stop writing isolated logic tests around mocks/fakes and make direct behavior tests cheap to add.

title: Cover SmartRunner happy-path handoff, resume, and auto-continue with direct fixtures
description: Add direct fixture-backed tests for SmartRunner success paths: rate-limit detection, fallback selection, auth swap, resume hint capture, resumed session id preservation, and auto-continue behavior. Focus on behaviors users see, not just helper functions.

title: Cover internal/exec failure branches with direct fixtures instead of doubles
description: Add fixture-backed tests for internal/exec failure branches: no usable fallback profiles, rollback behavior, stale buffered output after interruption, max-handoff exhaustion, notifier failure tolerance, and resume failure recovery. The deliverable is truthful behavior coverage for the runtime failure surface.

title: Burn down cmd/caam/cmd broad boundary exception with real CLI fixture flows
description: cmd/caam/cmd currently sits at 42.8% coverage and is blanket-allowlisted as boundary. Replace command-level mock filesystem/network assumptions with real temp-home, real profile-vault, and controlled subprocess fixtures so user-facing switching and status flows become true no-mock command tests where feasible.

title: Add temp-home command tests for run, resume, init, and shell startup paths
description: Use temp HOME/CAAM_HOME/CODEX_HOME layouts and real subprocess invocation to cover user-facing startup paths in cmd/caam/cmd: run, resume, init, and shell integration. Replace broad command-package fiction with direct command behavior proofs.

title: Add real vault and profile-store command tests for activate, status, sync, and project flows
description: Cover command flows that mutate or inspect real vault/profile/project state using temp directories instead of mocks. Focus on activate/status/sync/project surfaces because they are user-visible, stateful, and still under broad package-level realism exceptions.

title: Remove cmd/caam/cmd blanket boundary waiver in auditable slices
description: Burn down the broad cmd/caam/cmd realism exemption in slices tied to newly-added direct tests. Each removed slice must point to the exact tests that justify removal and leave only narrow, expiring exceptions where a real fixture is still genuinely blocked.

title: Remove realism debt from auth/profile/rate-limit core paths
description: Target the core user-dependent packages that are below a truthful full-coverage claim: internal/agent, authfile, authpool, identity, profile, refresh, rotation, and ratelimit. Convert tests to real-behavior fixtures, split adapter seams where necessary, and document only narrow exceptions with expiry and owner.

title: Close authfile, authpool, profile, and identity realism debt with real filesystem fixtures
description: Replace broad no-mock exemptions in authfile/authpool/profile/identity with real filesystem and real state fixtures. The point is to make core account-state behaviors certifiable without package-level fiction.

title: Close refresh, rotation, and ratelimit realism debt with direct behavior tests
description: Convert refresh/rotation/ratelimit tests toward direct behavior coverage using real config, real cooldown stores, and real transcripts instead of helper-only or fake-only paths. The deliverable is truthful coverage for account-selection and limit-detection logic.

title: Narrow remaining core-package exceptions with owner, expiry, and remediation links
description: After direct tests land, remove broad exceptions and leave only narrow waivers that name the exact construct still blocked, the owner, expiry, and the remediation bead that will remove it. No silent leftover broad exemptions.

title: Shrink package-wide allowlist entries into narrow expiring exceptions
description: Replace broad package prefixes in docs/testing/test_realism_allowlist.json with file- or construct-level exceptions that carry rationale, owner, expiry, and linked remediation tasks. The desired end state is that strict no-mock lint reflects reality instead of being satisfied by broad package exemptions.

title: Make strict no-mock realism lint authoritative and green
description: Once realism debt is burned down, wire scripts/lint_test_realism.sh --strict and its artifacts into CI as a fail-closed gate. The broad claim of no-mock unit coverage is not certifiable until this lane passes without package-wide escape hatches.

title: Complete E2E scenario coverage and reliability
description: Make the E2E suite complete, green, and representative of the real CAAM behaviors users depend on: rate-limit switching, active-session handoff, correct conversation resume, auto-continue after resume, daemon behavior, auth-change handling, installer/update flows, and TUI flows.

comments.author: AzureRidge
comments.text: This lane exists because the repo has many E2E tests and detailed step logging, but completeness is not yet true. Current evidence includes a failing daemon-signals workflow, open external E2E gaps recorded in docs/testing/e2e_gap_closure_rollup.md, and missing bounded-live proof for real exhausted-account switching plus correct-session resume-with-auto-continue.

comments.author: GreenBrook
comments.text: Decomposition note: this epic now separates the current full-suite blocker (TestDaemonSignals), the five named external E2E gap families, bounded-live exhausted-account proof, bounded-live resume-correct-conversation proof, and scenario-traceability closure. That sequencing makes it possible to see exactly which missing proof surfaces still block an honest completion claim.

title: Stabilize failing daemon signal workflow and remove cleanup flake
description: internal/e2e/workflows currently fails at TestDaemonSignals with TempDir cleanup / lingering profile artifact issues. Fix the startup, signal, shutdown, and cleanup behavior so the full E2E workflow package can run green and the audit scripts stop aborting on this known first failing condition.

title: Capture deterministic TestDaemonSignals first-failure evidence bundle
description: Make TestDaemonSignals reproducible with an evidence pack that captures the first failing condition, exact cleanup residue, replay command, and environment. This turns the current flaky cleanup symptom into a deterministic debugging target.

title: Fix daemon-signal lifecycle and temp-dir cleanup semantics
description: Repair the startup, signal, shutdown, and lingering-profile cleanup behavior so the daemon signal workflow no longer leaves non-empty temp directories behind. The outcome is a green and repeatable internal/e2e/workflows daemon-signal lane.

title: Add non-flaky regression proof for repeated daemon signal sequences
description: Add a regression run that replays the fixed daemon signal flow multiple times and proves cleanup stability under repeated signal sequences. This leaf closes the current known full-suite blocker instead of only hiding it.

title: Close named external E2E gaps: watcher, WezTerm recovery, distributed auth, installer/update, TUI
description: docs/testing/e2e_gap_closure_rollup.md still lists open external gap tasks for watcher/auth-change, WezTerm recovery, distributed auth smoke with verbose logs, installer/update, and TUI interaction. Implement or import those missing scenario scripts/tests so the scenario matrix can become truthful.

title: Add watcher auth-change E2E scripts and fixtures
description: Implement or import the missing watcher/auth-change E2E scenarios listed in the gap-rollup, with fixtures, assertions, and replay instructions. These tests need to count toward truthful scenario closure, not just exist as notes.

title: Add WezTerm recovery E2E scripts and fixtures
description: Implement the missing WezTerm recovery E2E coverage with fixtures and assertions that prove recovery behavior rather than only smoke-checking the CLI surface.

title: Add distributed-auth smoke scripts with verbose forensic logs
description: Implement the missing distributed-auth E2E smoke coverage and require verbose, machine-parseable logging so failure packets are diagnosable without reproducing the whole environment from scratch.

title: Add installer and update E2E scripts with replayable evidence
description: Implement missing installer/update E2E scenarios with replay commands, assertions, and artifacts that can be consumed by the truth audit and release gate.

title: Close TUI interaction E2E coverage gaps
description: Implement the missing TUI interaction E2E scenarios so user-visible TUI flows are represented in the truthful scenario inventory instead of remaining external open items.

title: Add bounded live exhausted-account switching validation
description: Add a bounded live validation run that starts a Codex session on an exhausted or deliberately rate-limited profile, verifies automatic selection of a healthy fallback profile, and records detailed evidence. This is required because current local smoke used a fake codex binary, not an actually exhausted real account.

title: Define bounded-live exhausted-account switching protocol and safety checklist
description: Write the bounded-live procedure for proving exhausted-account switching on a real account without uncontrolled blast radius. Capture account selection constraints, logging requirements, safety checks, and success/failure truth labels before running the live proof.

title: Run bounded-live exhausted-account auto-switch validation and capture full evidence
description: Execute the bounded-live exhausted-account switching proof and capture the triggering rate-limit event, selected fallback profile, command continuity, canonical logs, raw transcripts, and summary artifacts. This must be real evidence, not fake-binary substitution.

title: Normalize live exhausted-account proof into traceability and attestation artifacts
description: Convert the raw live exhausted-account evidence into the machine-readable artifacts consumed by traceability and release attestation so the proof counts toward truthful closure.

title: Add bounded live resume-correct-conversation and auto-continue validation
description: Prove with a bounded live run that CAAM resumes the correct Codex conversation after switching accounts and that the resumed session continues without waiting for the user to type go on/proceed. Capture the resumed session id, triggering rate-limit event, injected continuation prompt, and post-handoff transcript evidence.

title: Define bounded-live resume-correct-conversation protocol
description: Write the bounded-live procedure for proving that CAAM resumes the correct Codex conversation after account switching and does not silently drift into a different session. Require explicit resumed-session-id capture, continuation evidence, and safety limits.

title: Run live rate-limit handoff proving resumed session id and auto-continue behavior
description: Execute the bounded-live resume proof on a real rate-limited session and capture resumed session id, handoff trigger, continuation injection, and post-resume transcript evidence. This should directly answer whether CAAM resumes the right conversation and keeps working.

title: Normalize live resume proof into traceability and attestation artifacts
description: Convert the raw live resume evidence into traceability and attestation artifacts so the proof is durable, auditable, and counted correctly by the release gate.

title: Drive scenario traceability to truthful closure
description: Rebuild the CLI workflow matrix bindings, E2E inventory, and scenario traceability until required scenarios are either covered or explicitly accepted as out of scope. Treat stale or missing traceability as a blocking failure, not an informational doc gap.

title: Rebuild scenario-to-test bindings from current test names and workflow IDs
description: Refresh scenario_test_bindings using the current source tree and workflow IDs rather than stale assumptions. This is the prerequisite for any honest coverage claim because traceability cannot be trusted when the map is stale.

title: Close highest-risk uncovered runtime scenarios first
description: Take the current uncovered-scenario list and burn down the highest-risk runtime scenarios first: profile isolation, rotation cooldown, switching, resume continuity, and failure semantics users actually depend on.

title: Record explicit accepted-out-of-scope scenarios with rationale instead of silent gaps
description: Where a required scenario is intentionally out of scope, mark it explicitly with rationale and owner rather than leaving it as an unexplained gap. The traceability set should distinguish missing work from accepted non-goals.

title: Gate traceability totals consistency in CI and nightly runs
description: Make CI/nightly fail closed when required/covered/uncovered totals are inconsistent, stale, or missing. This prevents coverage docs from silently drifting away from the source tree.

title: Forensic logging and artifact completeness
description: Ensure every required E2E and live validation run emits machine-validated canonical logs plus human-usable evidence bundles. Current harnesses log richly, but validation and certification are not universal yet.

comments.author: AzureRidge
comments.text: Current E2E harnesses already emit rich summaries and step logs, but explicit canonical-log validation appears in only a narrow subset of switching tests. The gap is not zero logging; the gap is universal validated logging and artifact contracts that can support certification and fast debugging.

comments.author: GreenBrook
comments.text: Decomposition note: this epic now separates canonical-log enforcement, artifact-bundle completeness, and CI/nightly/release log gating. The goal is to make detailed forensic logging universal, validated, and certifiable rather than present only in some harnesses.

title: Require canonical log validation on every switching and session-handoff E2E
description: Today explicit ValidateCanonicalLogs calls are visible in rotation_exhaustion tests, but not uniformly enforced across the full switching/resume suite. Extend canonical log validation to every critical workflow that certifies switching, handoff, or resume behavior.

title: Enumerate every workflow that must call ValidateCanonicalLogs
description: Build the authoritative list of switching, handoff, resume, and bounded-live workflows that must perform canonical log validation. This creates the target set for hard fail-closed enforcement.

title: Add missing canonical validation hooks across required switching and resume E2Es
description: Wire ValidateCanonicalLogs or equivalent fail-closed validation into every workflow in the target set, including the newer switching and resume flows. The goal is universal enforcement, not selective validation on showcase tests.

title: Fail suites when required canonical validation is absent or bypassed
description: Add test or script-level enforcement that fails when a required workflow emits logs without canonical validation. This closes the loophole where logging exists but is never actually validated.

title: Standardize full forensic artifact bundle per E2E and live run
description: Every important run should emit: canonical JSONL, human-readable summary, raw terminal transcript, failure packet when relevant, correlation IDs, scenario id, owning component, and replay hints. Make the artifact contract explicit and machine-checkable.

title: Define a machine-checkable forensic artifact bundle schema
description: Specify the required artifact bundle fields for every important E2E or live run: canonical JSONL, human summary, raw transcript, scenario id, correlation ids, replay command, owner lane, and proof-class metadata. Make the contract explicit and parseable.

title: Emit raw transcript, canonical JSONL, summary, and replay hints on every important run
description: Update harnesses and scripts so every important E2E and bounded-live run emits the full artifact bundle instead of a partial or ad hoc subset. The goal is universal evidence completeness, not a few good examples.

title: Generate first-failure packets with scenario, owner, and replay context
description: Generate explicit first-failure packets that identify the first failing condition, scenario id, likely owner lane, artifact paths, and replay command. This makes failures actionable without manual archaeology.

title: Publish artifact index manifests consumed by attestation and dashboards
description: Produce a machine-readable artifact index for each run so later attestation and dashboard steps can consume evidence deterministically instead of scraping directories heuristically.

title: Gate schema, redaction, and timeline integrity for E2E logs in CI
description: Existing scripts validate sample fixtures, but the real suite should fail closed if emitted logs violate schema, correlation ordering, or redaction rules. Promote log-quality verification from sample-fixture validation to actual run validation.

title: Enforce schema and redaction validation on real emitted logs
description: Promote schema and redaction checks from sample-fixture validation to real emitted logs from the actual E2E and bounded-live workflows. Missing or invalid redaction/schema should fail the lane, not be advisory.

title: Enforce timeline and correlation ordering invariants on real runs
description: Validate that real emitted logs preserve ordering, correlation ids, and timeline integrity. This closes the gap where logs exist but cannot be trusted for forensic reconstruction.

title: Fail CI, nightly, and release flows when required log evidence is missing or invalid
description: Wire the schema/redaction/timeline checks into CI, nightly, and release surfaces so missing evidence or invalid logs block promotion rather than surfacing as best-effort warnings.

title: Generate human-readable first-failure debugging summaries from E2E artifacts
description: Produce concise incident-style summaries that identify the first failing condition, impacted scenario, likely owner lane, replay command, and evidence paths. This reduces operator time-to-understand when an E2E or live validation fails.

title: Fail-closed CI and certification gate
description: Publish a final gate that only certifies the broad testing claim when realism, coverage, E2E completeness, logging, and bounded live validation are all green simultaneously. No partial pass should be misreported as full closure.

comments.author: AzureRidge
comments.text: This lane prevents a repeat of the current mismatch between useful local tests and an overstated broad claim. Final certification must be fail-closed and label deterministic local proof, integration proof, and bounded live proof separately. Anything less than all required proof surfaces being green stays partial by design.

title: Separate deterministic local gate from bounded live gate with explicit truth labels
description: Do not let local deterministic success masquerade as live-production proof. Publish explicit labels for deterministic fixture proof, integration proof, and bounded live proof, and require all demanded categories before claiming full closure.

title: Publish auditable coverage, realism, E2E, and logging dashboards
description: Publish machine-readable and human-readable status artifacts that answer: current coverage by critical package, current realism exception count, current E2E required/covered/uncovered counts, and current logging validation status.

title: Final certification: allow full-coverage claim only after all proof surfaces are green
description: The closing certificate must require: strict realism gate pass, critical-package coverage closure, full E2E suite green, canonical logging validation across required flows, up-to-date traceability, and at least one full bounded live account-switch/resume proof run with detailed evidence. Anything less is partial, not full closure.
